cards.back: Package manager for Kubernetes — bundles K8s manifests into charts, manages releases, handles upgrades and rollbacks
A collection of files describing a K8s application: Chart.yaml (metadata), values.yaml (defaults), templates/ (manifests)
A deployed instance of a chart — one chart can be installed multiple times as different releases
Chart metadata: name, version, appVersion, description, dependencies. name sets .Chart.Name in templates
Default configuration values — overridden by -f values-prod.yaml or --set key=value at install time
install: first deployment. upgrade: update existing release. upgrade --install: does both (idempotent)
Renders chart templates locally without installing — useful for debugging and GitOps (render then apply)
Plugin that shows what would change before upgrading — like kubectl diff but for Helm releases
The name given to the release at install time — used to name resources uniquely
The namespace the release is deployed into — set by ArgoCD destination.namespace or --namespace flag
The name field from Chart.yaml — used to name Kubernetes resources in templates
Access values from values.yaml — e.g. .Values.replicaCount, .Values.image.tag
spec.source.helm.valueFiles: [values-gcp.yaml] — ArgoCD passes these to helm template at sync time
spec.source.helm.releaseName overrides the release name — controls .Release.Name and resource naming in templates
Plugin to encrypt values files with SOPS/GPG — decrypts at deploy time. Keeps secrets out of plaintext Git
List all releases in a namespace: helm list -A (all namespaces)
Show values used for a release: helm get values <release> -n <namespace>
Roll back to a previous revision: helm rollback <release> <revision> -n <namespace>
helm repo add <name> <url> — adds a chart repository. helm repo update — fetches latest charts
helm history <release> — shows all revisions with status, chart version, and description
